pattern: For each server c in a tree, count pairs of other servers (a, b) reachable through c where both path distances from c are divisible by signalSpeed; use DFS per subtree and multiply counts across subtrees.
hints: Root the tree at each candidate server c; for each subtree hanging off c, do a DFS counting how many nodes are at a distance divisible by signalSpeed from c.

If subtree i contributes cnt_i such nodes and we have already seen total_so_far from previous subtrees, the number of new valid pairs added is cnt_i * total_so_far; accumulate across all subtrees of c.
Edge weights are summed along the path; the condition is path_distance % signalSpeed == 0.

edge_inputs.description: two-node tree — only one edge, no valid pairs
star graph, all edges same weight divisible by signalSpeed
star graph with different weights — only some pairs qualify through center
signalSpeed=1 so all distances are divisible — pairs = left_size * right_size at each node
large weight not divisible by signalSpeed — no pairs

stress.description: random tree of 30 nodes with random edge weights and signalSpeed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        w = rng.randint(1, 20)
        edges.append([u, i, w])
    signalSpeed = rng.randint(1, 5)
    return [edges, signalSpeed]
